issues.title: Launch call: anonymous install ping + remote update check (one request, two jobs)

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

issues.body:
# Spec: one launch call that does two jobs

**For: `PipFoweraker/pdoom1`, in tonight's build if at all possible.**
Written 2026-07-22. Self-contained — hand this straight to the build.

## Why this is the one thing worth stopping for

Two problems, one HTTP call:

1. **You cannot count players.** The only game-to-server signal today is a score
   submission, which needs the player to finish a run AND not have opted out.
   `entry_uuid` is per-entry, so it cannot even dedupe people. v0.11.0 shows 135
   asset downloads on GitHub and there is no way to tell how many were humans
   rather than scanners — an install ID is the only thing that answers that.

2. **You cannot tell an existing player that a patch exists.** The game has
   `has_unseen_patch_notes()` in `godot/autoload/game_config.gd:368`, but it
   compares `last_seen_version != CURRENT_VERSION` — **both local**. That shows
   patch notes *after* someone updates; it cannot tell someone on v0.11.0 that
   v0.12.0 shipped. There is no remote version check anywhere in the Godot
   project.

Neither can be retrofitted onto builds already in players' hands. Every cohort
that downloads without this stays uncountable and unreachable **forever**. That
is the whole argument for the 36-hour window.

---

## The call

One request on launch. Fire-and-forget, async, never blocking startup.

### Request

```
POST https://analytics.pdoom1.com/api/event
Content-Type: application/json
User-Agent: <must be set to a real UA string; Plausible drops requests without one>

{
  "name": "Game Launch",
  "domain": "pdoom1.com",
  "url": "app://pdoom1/launch",
  "props": {
    "install_id":   "<uuid-v4>",
    "version":      "0.12.0",
    "os":           "Windows",
    "first_launch": true
  }
}
```

Verified live: the endpoint is up, `/api/health` reports Postgres and ClickHouse
both `ok`, and a test event returned `202`. **No new server code is needed.**

Plausible returns `202` for essentially anything, so a `202` means "accepted",
not "stored" — do not treat it as confirmation. Fire and forget; never retry in a
loop, never block on it.

### `install_id` rules

- UUIDv4, generated **once**, persisted to `user://`.
- **Never** derived from hardware, MAC, disk serial, username or anything else
  about the machine. A random UUID regenerated on reinstall is exactly right —
  it counts installs, which is what we want, and it is not a device fingerprint.
- Regenerating on reinstall is fine and expected. Do not try to make it survive.

### Consent

Gate it behind the existing privacy opt-out (the same one
`godot/autoload/leaderboard_sync.gd:85-91` already honours for score
submission). If the player has opted out, **do not send at all** — not an
anonymised version, nothing.

Reuse the async + durable-outbox pattern already proven in `leaderboard_sync.gd`.
Do not invent a second networking path.

---

## The update check (same launch, second job)

Read the current version from the website, which already publishes it:

```
GET https://pdoom1.com/data/version.json
```

```json
{ "latest_release": { "version": "v0.12.0", "published_at": "..." } }
```

That file is live now, auto-updated by a workflow, and served as static JSON off
DreamHost — no API, no auth, no rate limit worth worrying about.

Compare `latest_release.version` against the local `CURRENT_VERSION`. If the
remote is newer, show a non-blocking notice on the welcome screen:

> **v0.12.0 is available** — you're on v0.11.0. [Get it]

pointing at `https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/releases/latest`.

### Rules that matter

- **Never block startup or gameplay on this.** If the request fails, times out,
  or the JSON is malformed, show nothing and carry on silently. An update check
  that can break launch is worse than no update check.
- **Compare semver numerically, not as strings.** `"0.9.0" > "0.11.0"` is true
  for string comparison and would tell every v0.11.0 player they are ahead.
  Parse to integer triples.
- Tolerate the `v` prefix being present or absent on either side.
- Do not auto-download or auto-update. Show a notice and a link; the player
  decides.
- Make it dismissible, and don't re-nag every launch for the same version.

Note this is a plain static-file read with no identifiers attached — it carries
no privacy cost and does not need to sit behind the analytics opt-out. Keep the
two concerns separate in the code even though they happen at the same moment.

---

## Order of value if time is short

1. **`install_id` + launch ping** — un-retrofittable, and the only way to know
   whether those 135 downloads were people.
2. **Update check** — un-retrofittable *for this cohort*: players who install
   v0.12.0 without it can't be told about v0.13.0 either. Every release you ship
   without it extends the problem.
3. Everything else in `GAME_REPO_ASKS_ALPHA.md` (the bug-report panel's false
   "we'll create a GitHub issue soon" message, the `F8`-vs-`N` mismatch, the
   `/contributors` 404) can wait for the fast-follow.

If only one lands tonight, make it #1.

---

## What the website side already does, so you don't duplicate it

- Publishes `/data/version.json` with `latest_release.version`. Live.
- Accepts the launch ping at `analytics.pdoom1.com/api/event`. Live, returns 202.
- Counts arrivals and download clicks via Plausible. **Pip must add a `Download`
  Goal and allow the custom properties in Plausible settings before links go out
  — property allow-listing is not retroactive.**
- `scripts/alpha-watch.py` reads both streams and will start reporting install
  counts as soon as the ping exists.

Nothing on the website blocks any of this.

---

*Filed from `pdoom1-website`. Full spec also at `docs/GAME_SPEC_launch_ping_and_update_check.md` in that repo. Website side is ready and blocks none of this.*

issues.title: Finalize CREDITS.md (fill names) + merge after F&F deploy
issues.body:
Ready PR: #785 adds a root-level `CREDITS.md` (music-session close-out). Held unmerged so the in-progress 400mb friends-and-family build deploy cooks undisturbed.

## To close
1. After the deploy settles, review + merge #785.
2. Fill the **Notes for Pip** checklist in `CREDITS.md` (values calls only Pip can make):
   - [ ] preferred credited name/handle
   - [ ] friend's name/handle for the original tracks -- or "prefers anonymity" (deliberately not invented)
   - [ ] playtester acknowledgement(s)
   - [ ] AI-collaboration credit prominence
   - [ ] art-pipeline attributions (art lane)
   - [ ] decide whether an in-game credits screen is wanted (separate feature)
3. Confirm the friend's track-rights agreement in writing (per STEM_CATALOGUE.md).

Context: last artifact of Fable music session 1 (composed adaptive score shipped via #710, placeholders retired via #731).

issues.title: Put 'Buy Compute' under an operations-style submenu (action grouping)

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

issues.body: Fast-follow catch (Pip 2026-07-22, playing v0.12.0): the 'buy compute' action should live under one of the operations-style submenus, grouped with other operations actions, not loose. View-layer/action-grouping only -- no gameplay/ladder impact. Pointers: category grouping in main_ui.gd (category_order), the action's category field in godot/data/actions/*.json.

issues.title: Office cat 'Missy' (web-missy.jpg) does not render, though present + imported

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

issues.body: One of the 8 random office cats (Missy / assets/cats/simple/web-missy.jpg) does not render in the v0.12.0 build, while the other 7 do (Pip 2026-07-22, 'I swear I've seen her in other builds'). The file + its imported .ctex are present in the shipped tree, so likely a flaky import of that one JPG (re-import / re-encode web-missy.jpg). Low severity (1 of 8 random cats). office_cat.gd:12 loads from res://assets/cats/simple/. Not purge-related.

issues.title: Assign category-header icons to the PLAN action groups (hiring/resources/research/management/influence/...)

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

issues.body:
The proposed PLAN layout groups actions into high-level categories (main_ui.gd:1265: hiring, resources, research, funding, management, influence, strategic, other). They get category COLORS (ThemeManager.get_category_color) but no HEADER ICON was ever assigned -> placeholders in the build (Pip 2026-07-22). NOT a purge regression -- action-level _64 icons render fine; the category headers just have no icon.

Art-assignation task: generate/assign one icon per category group (8 icons) and wire them into the category-header rendering in main_ui. Fits the overnight art pipeline. Non-gameplay (view-layer).

issues.title: PLAN operations gantt: bigger font/area + a 'what's next / ETA to land' indicator
issues.body: Pip likes the proposed PLAN layout; the 'things in operation' (operations gantt / action queue) needs: (1) bigger font + more screen space, (2) an indicator of what's next and/or how long until it lands/arrives (ETA per queued op). Feeds the wave-two UI pass on the #778 proposed layout. 'A little to unpack, no immediate.'

issues.title: Office floor: all staff render as the same character + sprites oversized vs the room

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

issues.body: From Pip's v0.12.0 screenshot (2026-07-22): the office floor shows 3 distinct hires (Alex Anderson / Ellis Martinez / Gray Davis) but ALL render as the SAME woman sprite, and the characters are too LARGE relative to the office/surroundings. Researchers carry an appearance_id (body_00/body_03/body_20 etc.) that is not producing distinct sprites here. Two fixes: (1) map appearance_id -> distinct office_floor sprites (variety), (2) scale characters down to sit in the room. Pointers: office_floor.gd, the appearance/portrait pool. Ties to the office-sim pass + the representation/diversity standing rule.

issues.title: Hiring stitch: onboarding sub-actions as AP-sink prompts on offer-accept + interview schedule->happen flow

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

issues.body:
Early-game hiring is a priority to get right (Pip 2026-07-22, playing the A/B build). Two linked gaps:

1. **Onboarding sub-actions as prompts + predictable AP sinks.** Laptop-provisioning + mentoring for a new hire should surface as PROMPTS when a candidate ACCEPTS an offer during the turn, and act as predictable AP sinks you can plan for when you make the offer. Data already exists (researcher fields laptop_done/visa_done/mentoring_done/onboarded) -- it needs surfacing as costed actions.
2. **Interview = schedule -> happen.** Interviewing reads more like SCHEDULING an interview, then it taking place later. When the interview happens, fire a pop-up/notification (pairs with newness-glow, sibling issue).

Gameplay change (AP economy + timing) -> forks the ladder, fine at the 48h mark. Pointers: hiring pipeline, researcher.gd onboarding fields, month_plan/turn_manager (AP), NotificationManager.

issues.title: Early economy: force a small early lease spend; bedroom/basement start hard-caps hires; unlocks small-now, bigger-over-time
issues.body:
Progression idea (Pip 2026-07-22): first unlocks should have small immediate strategic impact, growing over time (switching costs). For the office:
- Very early, force a little spend: DECIDE ON A LEASE with starting money.
- Before that: bedroom/basement, SEVERELY capped # of hires (hard base limit).
- Lease unlocks more hires + capability; switching costs later.
- Rent falls due periodically -> predictable AP/cash sink.
Gameplay/progression -> forks the ladder (epoch/next-release). Pointers: finance_engine/ledger (rent as liability/AP sink), hiring cap on GameState, actions/data.

issues.title: Newness-glow: draw the eye to new/changed state (glow-until-mouseover)

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

issues.body: UX to be kind to players in early dev (Pip 2026-07-22): rather than punishing missed state changes, DRAW the eye to new/updated info with a subtle glow that persists until the player mouses over it, then clears. Use for interview results, new candidates, and other state changes (incl. the day/AP-driven ticks). Goal: new info is noticed, not missed, without nagging. View-layer/UX -- no gameplay/determinism change. Pairs with the hiring interview notification (sibling).

issues.title: UI consistency sweep after asset promotion + office-sim integration (level-2 visual pass)
issues.body:
Pip's forward-looking concern (2026-07-19), captured so it isn't lost.

## The two levels of the art pipeline
- **Level 1 (building now):** the review app's Keep/Reroll wiring PROMOTES asset files into `godot/assets/` and feeds Rerolls into the next generation wave. Makes assets *available*.
- **Level 2 (this issue):** the game UI must actually *reference and render* the promoted assets, the office simulator gets folded onto a real game screen, and then -- crucially -- a CONSISTENCY SWEEP over ALL UI elements.

## The sweep
Once the new assets are promoted + the office-sim is integrated + round-2 UI improvements land, audit every UI element/screen for visual coherence against the locked style: warm-grime + heavy-outline heft + deeper shadow; palette from `docs/art/palette.json`; consistent icon stroke/sizing; panel-frame treatment; spacing; the doom-as-additive-layer intensity rules. Goal: old and new assets don't clash; the whole game reads as one visual system.

## Sequencing (blocked-until)
Do the sweep AFTER: (a) assets promoted (level 1), (b) office-sim folded into a screen (see #663 + the OfficeFloor integration task), (c) round-2 UI improvements. Approach: inventory all UI elements -> map each to current vs intended asset -> flag inconsistencies -> apply the style guide uniformly.

Not blocking the v0.11.0 alpha release (which ships with current UI). This is the visual-coherence capstone for the art push.

issues.title: Leaderboard: mark runs where dev mode was used (visible badge, NOT exclusion)

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

issues.body:
Lightweight transparency marker + filter (Pip 2026-07-22): players should experiment a LOT with the dev tools; we do NOT exclude or auto-reject dev-assisted runs. We MARK a run where dev mode fired > 0 times, and let viewers filter them out on demand. Honest board, zero policing.

## Mechanism
- Set a `dev_mode_used` bool the instant any dev-overlay function is invoked (route dev-overlay mutations through one 'mark this run dev-touched' path -- today dev_mode_overlay.gd pokes state directly, e.g. :279 sets state.doom, bypassing the tracked pipeline).
- Carry it in the score submission + the VerificationTracker export artifact (add the server field versioned-never-silent, ADR-0002).

## Display (both are just client-side reads of the one flag)
- A small marker / colour highlight on flagged leaderboard entries (and the game-over screen). The score STILL posts and ranks -- it just wears a badge.
- A 'Hide dev-mode runs' filter toggle on the leaderboard screen that collapses the board to legit-only runs on demand. Default: show all (badges visible); toggle hides the flagged ones.

## Explicitly NOT this (per Pip)
- No segregation, no exclusion, no auto-rejection.
- Robust replay-verification (re-simulate the recorded log from seed, reject non-reproducing runs) is a SEPARATE, LATER item. For now we trust self-disclosure and keep the barrier to experimentation at zero.

Why: dev functions bypass the action/replay pipeline, so today a dev-assisted score posts with no marker at all. Badge + optional filter make the board honest without gatekeeping.

issues.title: Repo tidy: purge shippable cruft from godot/ to shrink the .pck (+ front-door README rewrites)

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

issues.body:
Fast-follow build-hygiene (non-gameplay -> no ladder fork; ships as a build-bump patch). Bumped to the 48h train per Pip (the v0.12.0 upload/download pain is the direct symptom).

## Why
Godot packs the ENTIRE godot/ tree into the shipped .pck (referenced or not), so scratch/retired assets living inside godot/ bloat both the friend download AND the release-upload time. The v0.12.0 .pck is ~400 MB.

## Do
- Move retired/source assets OUT of godot/ (to archive/ or art_source/) so they stop packing -- headline: godot/assets/dump_october_31_2025/ (scratch dump). **Grep `uid://` before moving ANY asset** -- Godot 4 scenes reference assets by UID, not path, so a blind move silently breaks scene refs.
- Untrack the ~220 MB stale builds/windows/v0.10.1 packs (git rm --cached; plan off-git storage).
- Rewrite the front-door READMEs: godot/README.md (still describes the removed Python bridge) and root README.md (incl. the pre-Godot screenshot on the GitHub landing page).
- Archive-not-delete anything with evolutionary/blog value (stray legacy/, stale shared_bridge-referencing docs) per the archival policy.

## Refs
Cruft audit (2026-07-22 session). Also fold the 'Godot packs all of godot/; grep uid:// before moving' rule into CLAUDE.md build guidance as part of this PR.

issues.title: docs: CREDITS.md (music close-out) -- READY, hold merge until deploy cooks
issues.body:
Root-level `CREDITS.md`. Sits outside `godot/`, so it does **not** ship in the .pck (no bloat to the release build).

**DO NOT MERGE until the 400mb build deploy finishes** -- Pip wants that to cook undisturbed. Nothing here touches main until you merge; new file, zero conflict risk.

Filled: game/engine, the composed adaptive score (Pip directing, composed with Claude/Fable in Strudel), the friend's retired original tracks acknowledged, tools.

Left for Pip (values calls, in the "Notes for Pip" checklist at the bottom of the file):
- [ ] your preferred credited name/handle
- [ ] your friend's name/handle -- or "prefers anonymity" (deliberately not invented; unnamed in-repo)
- [ ] playtester credit
- [ ] AI-credit prominence (wording is honest-and-plain; dial to taste)
- [ ] art-pipeline attributions (art lane's to fill)
- [ ] whether to build an in-game credits screen (separate feature)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: docs(music): session-1 closeout STATE block (orientation header only)
issues.body:
Last artifact from the Fable music session -- pure orientation, no taste rulings changed.

Adds a **STATE** block at the top of `tools/music/DIRECTION_NOTES.md` (the read-first music doc) so a future session sees status immediately instead of scrolling past months of append-only history:
- what shipped (composed 5-tier score + cues, all on main, placeholders retired)
- the pipeline in 4 lines (compose -> jukebox judge -> capture_takes -> process_captures -> wire)
- where session 2 resumes (in-game listen verdict; the humans-go-metal casting question; real-stem recording; benched recyclable takes)
- one flagged open item for you: **no game CREDITS file exists** -- the composed score and your friend's retired tracks both want acknowledgement (your values call, not mine to write)

Safe to merge whenever -- or let the orchestrator fold it into the release tidy. Zero code, one doc file.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
